BuyOrder.title: BuyOrder
BuyOrder.description: Represents a user's buy order for USDT.

id.description: Unique identifier for the buy order.

network.description: The selected network for the USDT transaction (BEP20, TRC20, ERC20).

usdtAmount.description: The amount of USDT the user wants to buy.

inrAmount.description: The equivalent amount in INR for the USDT purchase.

usdtAddress.description: The user's USDT address to receive the purchased USDT.

paymentMode.description: The selected payment mode (Cash Deposit, UPI, IMPS, NEFT, RTGS).

paymentInformation.description: Payment information based on the selected payment method.

paymentToken.description: Unique token generated for the payment.

orderStatus.description: The status of the order (e.g., pending, processing, completed, cancelled).

SellOrder.title: SellOrder
SellOrder.description: Represents a user's sell order for USDT.

id.description: Unique identifier for the sell order.

network.description: The selected network for the USDT transaction (BEP20, TRC20, ERC20).

usdtAmount.description: The amount of USDT the user wants to sell.

inrAmount.description: The equivalent amount in INR for the USDT sale.

paymentReceivingMode.description: The selected payment receiving mode (Cash Deposit, UPI, IMPS, RTGS, NEFT).

upiHolderName.description: The name of the UPI account holder (if UPI is selected).

upiId.description: The UPI ID (if UPI is selected).

bankName.description: The name of the bank for receiving payment (if not UPI).

accountNumber.description: The bank account number for receiving payment (if not UPI).

ifscCode.description: The IFSC code of the bank for receiving payment (if not UPI).

orderStatus.description: The status of the order (e.g., pending, processing, completed, cancelled).

id.description: Unique identifier for the payment method.

methodName.description: Name of the payment method (e.g., UPI, IMPS, NEFT).

qrCodeImage.description: URL or path to the QR code image for the payment method.

accountHolderName.description: Account holder name for the payment method (if applicable).

bankName.description: Bank name for the payment method (if applicable).

accountNumber.description: Account number for the payment method (if applicable).

ifscCode.description: IFSC code for the payment method (if applicable).

ContactMessage.title: ContactMessage
ContactMessage.description: Represents a contact message submitted by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the contact message.

userName.description: The name of the user submitting the message.

userEmail.description: The email address of the user submitting the message.

problemDescription.description: A description of the user's problem or inquiry.

submissionDate.description: The date and time the message was submitted.

AppSettings.title: Application Settings
AppSettings.description: Stores global application settings managed by the admin.

definition.description: Stores user's buy orders. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their orders.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who created the buy order.

params.name: buyOrderId
params.description: The ID of the buy order document.

definition.description: Stores user's sell orders. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their orders.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who created the sell order.

params.name: sellOrderId
params.description: The ID of the sell order document.

definition.description: Stores payment method configurations. Publicly readable.

params.name: paymentMethodId
params.description: The ID of the payment method document.

definition.description: Stores contact messages submitted by users.

params.name: contactMessageId
params.description: The ID of the contact message document.

definition.description: Stores global application settings. This should be a single document, e.g., /settings/appSettings. Publicly readable, admin writable.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore data structure is designed to prioritize authorization independence, clarity, and scalability, aligning with the core principles and strategy mandates.  User-owned data, such as BuyOrders and SellOrders, are stored within user-specific subcollections (`/users/{userId}/buyOrders` and `/users/{userId}/sellOrders`), ensuring clear ownership and simplifying security rules. This structure inherently supports Authorization Independence because access control is path-based, avoiding the need for `get()` calls to parent documents. PaymentMethods are stored in a top-level collection (`/paymentMethods`) and are publicly readable, as they represent system-wide configurations. ContactMessages are stored in the `/contactMessages` collection. To support QAPs (Rules Are Not Filters), the path-based ownership of BuyOrders and SellOrders ensures secure listing, as rules can easily filter by `request.auth.uid`. The structure uses descriptive naming conventions for collections and documents to maximize debuggability. No denormalization is necessary as all data is user specific. The admin section's data (payment methods) doesn't require complex authorization; therefore, no specific denormalization is needed.
